text_prompt: A medium close-up holds a middle-aged woman at shoulder height in the corner of a small laundromat as she presses her palm to a fogged glass panel, her tired eyes lowered and her breath barely clouding the surface. Flat overcast light washes the frame in muted blue-gray tones, catching the tight texture of her closely braided hair, the worn seams of her denim jacket, and the small scar near one eyebrow while the background sinks into soft blur. She keeps her hand there for a beat, fingertips spreading slightly on the damp glass, then her shoulders loosen and she slowly turns toward the camera until one eye comes fully into focus and the moisture on her skin and lashes becomes visible. The camera stays locked and intimate, so the turn reveals only more of her face and the shallow space behind her: a pale wall, a soft rectangle of window light, and indistinct machine edges. A weak ventilation fan drones overhead, distant footsteps drift down the hall, and just as she parts her lips to speak, a nearby door clicks shut off screen and she stills mid-breath.
